decks.notes: This deck focuses on using mana acceleration to quickly ramp into powerful Eldrazi creatures and control spells. It utilizes cards like Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple to generate additional mana, allowing you to play threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Ugin, Eye of the Storms ahead of schedule. The deck also features removal spells like Fatal Push and Kozilek's Command to control the board. The key strategy is to overwhelm your opponent with big creatures while disrupting their game plan with powerful control elements.

The main idea of this UW Control deck is to utilize a mix of counterspells, removal, card advantage, and planeswalkers to control the game and outvalue the opponent. Using cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Narset, Parter of Veils to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage with spells like Orim's Chant and Consult the Star Charts. The deck aims to control the board and slow down the opponent's game plan until it can establish a dominant position and win the game.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay with a mixture of powerful creatures backed up by burn spells and disruptive cards. It aims to quickly put pressure on the opponent by playing efficient threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer, while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Blood Moon and Goblin Bombardment. The deck has a solid mana base with fetch lands and powerful finishers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. By combining fast creatures, burn spells, and disruptive elements, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo using cards like Basking Broodscale, Glaring Fleshraker, and Blade of the Bloodchief to create a dominant board presence. It utilizes support cards such as Ancient Stirrings and Malevolent Rumble to search for key pieces and control the game. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Urza's Saga and Boseiju, Who Endures to protect its strategy. With a mix of ramp, removal, and combo pieces, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to apply early pressure on the opponent. With cards like Galvanic Discharge and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah providing removal and additional damage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. Ranger-Captain of Eos helps tutor for essential creatures, while Seasoned Pyromancer provides card advantage and tokens. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Blood Moon and Solitude to further disrupt the opponent's game plan and secure victory through relentless aggression.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly through rituals and mana-producing spells to cast big spells like Past in Flames and Ral, Monsoon Mage. It also utilizes cards like Reckless Impulse and Wrenn's Resolve to draw through the deck and find key combo pieces. The deck aims to chain spells together to deal massive damage with spells like Artist's Talent and Hex Magic. The use of Wish allows for flexibility in finding specific cards from the sideboard to further enhance the combo potential. The deck is designed to quickly overwhelm opponents with its explosive synergy.

This Modern Ruby Storm deck focuses on casting multiple spells in quick succession to generate a large amount of mana and then finish off the opponent with a powerful combo involving Ral, Monsoon Mage, Past in Flames, and Grapeshot. The deck includes cards like Manamorphose and Pyretic Ritual to ramp up mana production and Ruby Medallion to reduce the cost of spells. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to respond.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats on the board, and utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Teferi to gain advantage. The deck also aims to generate card advantage with cards like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive. Additionally, the inclusion of Isochron Scepter allows for repeatable spell casting. The mana base is optimized for consistency with fetch lands and dual lands. Overall, the deck seeks to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing control and dominance on the battlefield.

The deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures using cards like Street Wraith and Waker of Waves, then cast Living End to return all those creatures to the battlefield. This powerful combo allows for a massive swing in tempo, overwhelming the opponent with a board full of creatures. The deck also includes disruption and card draw with cards like Subtlety and Shardless Agent, ensuring that the combo can be executed effectively. Overall, the key strategy of the deck is to set up and execute the Living End combo for a game-winning play.

The deck focuses on quickly getting Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play using Goryo's Vengeance or Ephemerate to cheat it out, along with other creatures like Griselbrand or Solitude. It uses disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo and control the opponent's game plan. The deck also has a toolbox of removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Wrath of the Skies to deal with threats. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disrupt their strategy until victory is achieved.

This deck focuses on utilizing instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to quickly bring powerful creatures like Griselbrand onto the battlefield. The deck also includes cards like Solitude and Faithful Mending to control the board and gain card advantage. By combining reanimation spells with creatures that have strong enter-the-battlefield effects, the deck aims to create explosive and unexpected plays that can overwhelm opponents in a quick and efficient manner. The inclusion of cards like Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog adds another layer of disruption and control to the strategy.

The main idea of this deck is to assemble the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. This allows you to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and control spells such as Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. The deck focuses on disrupting your opponent's game plan with counterspells and removal while establishing a strong board presence with big creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Emrakul, the Promised End. The goal is to overwhelm your opponent with powerful threats and control elements.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the Amulet of Vigor to generate extra mana, allowing for explosive plays with cards like Primeval Titan and Scapeshift. By utilizing lands like Gruul Turf and Simic Growth Chamber, the deck can quickly ramp up its mana production and search for key pieces like Urza's Saga and Boseiju, Who Endures. The deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and land interactions, ultimately leading to a decisive victory.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Arboreal Grazer and Amulet of Vigor, allowing for powerful plays like casting Primeval Titan early on. The deck also incorporates land manipulation cards like Scapeshift and Simic Growth Chamber to generate even more mana and summon powerful creatures like Cultivator Colossus. With the ability to search for key cards using Green Sun's Zenith and Tolaria West, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a massive board presence and finish the game with powerful combo plays.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing low-curve creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu to apply early pressure. The inclusion of powerful cards like Scion of Draco and Kroxa, Titan of Death's Hunger allows for additional late-game reach and resilience. The deck also features a strong removal suite with cards like Fatal Push and Lightning Bolt to control the board and clear the way for attacks. The mana base is flexible and supports the deck's multi-color requirements effectively.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly through rituals and mana accelerants, while using cards like Ruby Medallion and Ral, Monsoon Mage to reduce the cost of spells and generate card advantage. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces needed for an explosive turn where multiple spells can be cast in a single turn, potentially leading to a win through a big Grapeshot or Past in Flames recursion. The deck seeks to outpace opponents and win before they can establish their game plan.
